title: Editas Medicine Announces New Safety and Efficacy Data from EdiTHAL Trial in Beta Thalassemia
text: Editas Medicine announced new safety and efficacy data from EdiTHAL trial of reni-cel in 7 patients with transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia presented at European Hematology Association annual congress. Patients demonstrated early and robust total hemoglobin and fetal hemoglobin increases with follow-up periods spanning 4 to 12 months. Total hemoglobin rose above transfusion independence threshold of 9 g/dL, representing significant clinical improvement for patients requiring regular blood transfusions. Treatment well-tolerated with safety profile consistent with conditioning regimen. Successful engraftment demonstrated in all patients with durable therapeutic benefit sustained over follow-up period. Data supports potential of reni-cel as transformative one-time treatment option for transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia.
